Sub-problem 1:  
The number of rooms requiring attention follows a Poisson distribution with a mean of 20. The probability that exactly 15 rooms need attention is calculated using the Poisson probability formula:  
P(X = 15) = (e^(-20) * 20^15) / 15!  
The result is approximately 0.0153  

Sub-problem 2:  
The concierge assists with 8 concerns and has a success rate of 90%. The probability that at least 7 of these concerns are resolved successfully is the sum of the probabilities of resolving exactly 7 and exactly 8 concerns.  
Using the binomial distribution formula:  
P(X = 7) = C(8 7) * (0.9)^7 * (0.1)^1  
P(X = 8) = C(8 8) * (0.9)^8 * (0.1)^0  
Adding these probabilities gives approximately 0.3826
